metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate in AC4 (or a sub-bullet) the specific new test assertions that must be added to `test_metrics_docs.py` — at minimum: one assertion that `docs/reuse-surface.md` contains `scc` and `jscpd` as PATH prereqs, one that `docs/config.md` contains `[code_health]`, and one that `docs/user-guide.md` contains the three-part install instruction — so the prove command `pytest -q tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py` is not passable without those assertions being present.
evidence: AC4 PROVE: `pytest -q tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py` with no specification of which new assertions must be added

Existing test file (tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py) contains only: test_janitor_discovery_cites_rebar_metrics, test_user_guide_has_metrics_section, test_reuse_surface_references_metrics_registry, test_agents_where_to_read_has_metrics_pointer — none assert metrics extra, scc/jscpd prereqs, or code_health config keys
Approach states 'extend the existing docs test' but names no new assertion targets
findings.finding: AC4's PROVE command (`pytest -q tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py`) passes trivially on the pre-existing 121b assertions without any new assertions being added, because none of the four existing test functions check for `[metrics]` extra presence in docs, scc/jscpd PATH-prereq text in reuse-surface.md, `[code_health]` key in config.md, or three-part install copy in user-guide.md. The plan says 'extend the existing docs test' but the prove command cannot distinguish zero new assertions from a full extension.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — AC4 / Approach section ('extend the existing docs test rather than adding a parallel one')
scenarios: Implementer runs existing tests (all green pre-change), writes no new assertions; AC4 prove passes; docs content for [metrics] extra, scc/jscpd, and [code_health] keys is never tested

findings.finding: The test extension spec in AC4 does not enumerate what new assertions must be added to test_metrics_docs.py, leaving the verification step structurally unable to cover the new content deliverables in AC1+AC2 (docs/config.md [code_health] section, scc/jscpd as PATH prereqs in reuse-surface.md, lizard-only [metrics] extra, analyzer seam). The existing test file (confirmed by read) checks only the four 121b assertions; none of them touch config.md or the metrics extra/PATH prereqs. A passing test run after this ticket would NOT prove AC1+AC2 content is correct.
findings.location: AC4: 'tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py extended; existing assertions still pass' and Verification: 'test_metrics_docs.py passes (old + new assertions)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'New assertions must cover: (a) docs/config.md contains `[code_health]` and at least the keys `size_cap`/`size_near_fraction`; (b) docs/reuse-surface.md contains `scc` and `jscpd` as PATH prerequisites; (c) docs/reuse-surface.md names `lizard` as the only pip dep in the `[metrics]` extra; (d) docs/reuse-surface.md references the analyzer seam (AnalyzerResult or guard_import).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC1's proving command to also assert jscpd presence, e.g. `grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "jscpd" docs/reuse-surface.md`.
evidence: AC1 text: 'documents the analyzer seam + [metrics] extra (lizard) + scc/jscpd as separate PATH prereqs'
AC1 prove: 'grep -Eq "metrics\\]" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md'
jscpd is absent from the grep command
findings.finding: AC1's proving command checks for 'scc' but not 'jscpd', even though the criterion explicitly requires both scc AND jscpd to be documented as PATH prereqs — the second prereq is entirely unverified by the grep.
scenarios: An executor documents lizard and scc but omits jscpd entirely; AC1 passes green while the criterion is only half-met.
findings.suggested_fix: PROVE: `grep -Eq 'metrics\]' docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q 'scc' docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q 'jscpd' docs/reuse-surface.md`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the expected [code_health] keys in the plan and strengthen AC2's proving command to spot-check at least one key-level entry (e.g. grep for a representative key name), so the command cannot pass on a bare section header alone.
evidence: AC2 criterion: 'docs/config.md documents every [code_health] key with type + default'
AC2 prove: 'grep -q "\\[code_health\\]" docs/config.md'
A file containing only the header '## [code_health]' passes the grep but documents zero keys.
findings.finding: AC2's proving command (`grep -q "\[code_health\]" docs/config.md`) only verifies the section header exists, not that every [code_health] key has a type and default documented — the gap between the stated criterion and the measurable proof is wide enough that the command can pass while the requirement is unmet.
scenarios: Executor adds the section header but omits individual key entries; CI is green while the canonical config reference is incomplete.
Reviewer cannot tell from the AC whether 3 keys or 30 keys are expected, making completeness unverifiable.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a grounded-facts line listing the [code_health] keys (discoverable from the config schema/source); update AC2's prove to e.g. `grep -q 'enabled' docs/config.md && grep -q '\[code_health\]' docs/config.md` using a key name that must appear.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AC1 PROVE command to also grep for `jscpd` in docs/reuse-surface.md (e.g. append `&& grep -q "jscpd" docs/reuse-surface.md`) so both PATH prereqs are mechanically enforced.
evidence: AC1 criterion text: 'scc/jscpd as separate PATH prereqs'
AC1 PROVE: `grep -Eq "metrics\\]" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md`
No `grep -q "jscpd"` in the prove chain — jscpd coverage is entirely unenforced
findings.finding: AC1's PROVE command (`grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md`) only checks for `scc` but the criterion explicitly requires both `scc` AND `jscpd` to be documented as separate PATH prereqs. The enforcement mechanism silently passes if jscpd is omitted from the docs.
scenarios: Implementer adds scc to docs/reuse-surface.md but forgets jscpd; AC1 PROVE passes green; jscpd prereq silently missing from adoption docs
findings.suggested_fix: PROVE: `grep -Eq "metrics\\]" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "jscpd" docs/reuse-surface.md`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction between 'documentation of the shipped surface' and the fact that the `metrics` extra is added by ticket 9597 (not yet shipped): either update the Description to say 'documents the surface being shipped by 9597' or add 9597 as an explicit dependency alongside 0734.
evidence: Description: 'documentation of the shipped surface'
Grounded facts: 'today the extras are mcp/reviewbot/ui/agents/eval/tracing/grounding/grounding-t2/dev — there is NO `metrics` extra yet; it is ADDED by ticket 9597 (depends chain)'
Dependencies section: 'Dependencies: 0734 (content prerequisite; relates_to recorded)' — 9597 is absent
findings.finding: The Description characterises this ticket as 'documentation of the shipped surface,' but the Grounded Facts explicitly state the `metrics` extra 'is ADDED by ticket 9597 (depends chain)' and does not yet exist. The Dependencies section lists only 0734, not 9597. An implementer reading the Description will believe the surface is already live, while the Grounded Facts reveal a prerequisite ticket (9597) that is untracked in Dependencies — creating contradictory signals about readiness.
findings.location: Description vs Grounded Facts vs Dependencies section
scenarios: An implementer may start writing docs before 9597 lands, documenting an extra that doesn't exist in pyproject.toml yet.
A reviewer verifying 'shipped surface' will be confused when grepping pyproject.toml and not finding `[metrics]`.
findings.suggested_fix: Change Description to: 'Document the code-health surface being shipped by 9597 for client adoption.' Add `9597` to the Dependencies line: 'Dependencies: 9597 (metrics extra prerequisite), 0734 (content prerequisite).'

evidence: verify command `head -1 docs/config.md` inspects output with `head` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a second grep to AC3's proving command that asserts the fallback/Unavailable behavior is documented, e.g. append `&& grep -qi 'unavailable' docs/user-guide.md`.
evidence: AC3 criterion: 'user-guide documents the three-part install + "no analyzer -> Unavailable" behavior'
AC3 prove: 'grep -q "rebar\\[metrics\\]" docs/user-guide.md'
The string 'Unavailable' (or equivalent) is not part of the grep assertion.
findings.finding: AC3's proving command (`grep -q "rebar\[metrics\]" docs/user-guide.md`) only verifies the install string is present, leaving the second half of the criterion — '"no analyzer -> Unavailable" behavior' — without any proving command, so the fallback contract can be undocumented while AC3 passes green.
scenarios: Executor documents the install instructions thoroughly but forgets the fallback behavior section; AC3 passes while the fallback contract is absent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 0734 must land (depends_on) before this documentation ticket executes, or explicitly note that the `cap-change events` reference in `docs/user-guide.md` line 251 must be removed as part of this ticket's scope when 0734's deregistration lands.
evidence: Ticket 0734 description: 'DEREGISTER the spec from the portable registry (remove from register()), documented in the ticket-close note'
docs/user-guide.md line 251: 'code_health (module-size distribution and trend vs the locked cap, churn, refactor-to-addition ratio, cap-change events)'
The dep edge is relates_to (not depends_on), confirmed in ticket 0734 deps list: relates_to a9dd-041f-de89-48c5
A relates_to edge is advisory, not blocking — this ticket can execute without waiting for 0734
findings.finding: The plan asserts that dep 0734 is a 'content prerequisite' whose execution is required before documentation can be written, yet the recorded dependency edge is `relates_to` (not `depends_on`). Ticket 0734 DEREGISTERS `cap_change_events` from the metrics registry, but `docs/user-guide.md` line 251 currently documents `code_health` as including 'cap-change events'. If this documentation ticket executes before 0734 lands, the author will document a metric that is being removed — or worse, the author may not realize the user-guide already contains stale content about a metric this ticket's prerequisite removes.
findings.location: Grounded facts / Dependencies section — 'Dependencies: 0734 (content prerequisite; relates_to recorded)'
scenarios: Author writes new `docs/user-guide.md` content citing `cap_change_events` as part of the code_health lens, unaware 0734 removes it — the doc is born stale.
Author removes the existing `cap-change events` reference from user-guide.md to align with 0734's removal, but 0734 hasn't landed yet — the doc contradicts the live code.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the dependency edge from `relates_to` to `depends_on` for 0734, or add an explicit AC item: 'Remove the `cap-change events` reference from the code_health lens description in `docs/user-guide.md` (aligning with 0734's deregistration of that metric)' with a proving PROVE grep that the phrase is absent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an Acceptance Criterion covering the AGENTS.md change — e.g. 'AGENTS.md documents the metrics-surface scope and any agent guidance; PROVE: `grep -q "metrics" AGENTS.md`' — or remove AGENTS.md from Files if no change is actually needed.
evidence: Files: 'docs/reuse-surface.md, docs/config.md, docs/user-guide.md, AGENTS.md, tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py (extend)'
ACs 1–4 each target docs/reuse-surface.md, docs/config.md, docs/user-guide.md, and test_metrics_docs.py respectively — AGENTS.md has no corresponding AC
findings.finding: AGENTS.md is listed in the Files section as a deliverable to be modified, but no Acceptance Criterion covers it — it is an uncovered deliverable with no observable prove step.
scenarios: An implementer may skip AGENTS.md changes entirely (no AC to fail).
A reviewer has no observable signal to verify AGENTS.md was updated correctly.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '- [ ] AGENTS.md is updated to document agent guidance for the metrics/code_health surface. PROVE: `grep -q "code_health" AGENTS.md`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion + PROVE command for AGENTS.md (e.g., `grep -q 'scc' AGENTS.md`) so its required content is gated like the other doc files.
evidence: Files list: 'docs/reuse-surface.md, docs/config.md, docs/user-guide.md, AGENTS.md, tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py (extend)'
ABSENCE: no AC line mentioning AGENTS.md, no PROVE: grep targeting it
findings.finding: AGENTS.md is listed in the Files section but has no corresponding acceptance criterion or proving command, leaving its content ungated and verifiable only by human review.
findings.location: Files section: 'AGENTS.md' + Acceptance criteria (no AGENTS.md AC)
scenarios: AGENTS.md update is forgotten or done incompletely because no gate catches it
Future reviewers cannot confirm what was required for AGENTS.md, leading to inconsistent content
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC line such as: '- [ ] AGENTS.md documents the scc/jscpd PATH prereqs for metrics. PROVE: `grep -q "scc" AGENTS.md`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC (with a PROVE command or pytest assertion) for every change made to AGENTS.md, or remove AGENTS.md from the Files list if no change is actually required.
evidence: Plan lists 'AGENTS.md' in Files but none of the four ACs (reuse-surface grep, config.md grep, user-guide grep, pytest) reference or verify any AGENTS.md change.
All other listed files have at least one PROVE command or a pytest assertion; AGENTS.md has none.
findings.finding: AGENTS.md is listed in the Files scope but has no acceptance criterion that specifies what must be written there or proves the change is correct. This leaves the file's intended content unverified and creates a silent scope item that the test suite (pytest on test_metrics_docs.py) does not cover.
findings.location: Files: docs/reuse-surface.md, docs/config.md, docs/user-guide.md, AGENTS.md, tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py (extend)
scenarios: An implementer adds or omits AGENTS.md changes; neither outcome is caught by any AC or test, so the PR could merge with stale, missing, or incorrect agent-guidance content without failing any check.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add an AC such as 'AGENTS.md records the [metrics] extra and PATH-CLI prereqs for agent consumers. PROVE: grep -q "metrics" AGENTS.md' and cover it in test_metrics_docs.py, or (b) drop AGENTS.md from the Files list if no update is needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `examples/agent-skills/rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md` to the Files section (read-only dependency of AC4's prove command) so executors know it is a live test dependency and can assess whether new assertions require updating it.
evidence: Plan Files section lists: docs/reuse-surface.md, docs/config.md, docs/user-guide.md, AGENTS.md, tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py (extend) — no mention of examples/agent-skills/rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md
test_metrics_docs.py line 24: text = _read("examples/agent-skills/rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md")
File exists and already satisfies existing assertions (lines 41-46 of discovery.md contain 'rebar metrics' and 'unavailable'), so existing AC4 pre-existing assertions pass without modification
If the plan's new test assertions extend coverage to this file (e.g. asserting [metrics] extra install instructions appear there), the omission from Files becomes a blocker
findings.finding: examples/agent-skills/rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md is a live file read by the existing test_janitor_discovery_cites_rebar_metrics() assertion in tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py (the AC4 prove command), but it is not listed in the plan's Files section. An executor extending the test with new assertions that target this file would have no plan-level cue that edits to it are in scope or that the file exists as a live dependency.
scenarios: Executor adds new assertions to test_metrics_docs.py that check janitor/phases/discovery.md for metrics-extra install guidance, but doesn't update the file because it isn't in the Files list — new assertions fail.
Reviewer scanning Files section to assess scope misses this file as an implicit test dependency.
findings.suggested_fix: Append `examples/agent-skills/rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md (test dependency — read by existing assertion; update only if new assertions require it)` to the Files list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add ticket e7a0 (the `[code_health]` config schema ticket) to the Dependencies list, or explicitly state in AC2 where the implementer should read the authoritative key inventory (e.g. 'see ticket e7a0 for the full key list').
evidence: AC2: 'docs/config.md documents every `[code_health]` key with type + default'
Plan Dependencies: '0734 (content prerequisite)' — e7a0 not listed

Ticket 0734 itself has deps: 3b30, 18f9, 21fb, 3ba0 — e7a0 is a separate ticket that defines the code_health config schema (confirmed via cache finds referencing 'scan_roots', 'enabled', 'size_cap', 'size_near_fraction')
docs/config.md currently contains zero references to `code_health` (confirmed by search)
findings.finding: AC2 requires documenting 'every `[code_health]` key with type + default' in docs/config.md, but the canonical definition of those keys lives in ticket e7a0 (a transitive dependency of dep 0734, not listed in this plan's Dependencies). The plan provides no mechanism for the implementer to discover the correct key inventory — they must navigate an unlisted dep, introducing a silent discovery gap.
scenarios: Implementer reads only 0734 and writes incomplete or incorrect [code_health] key documentation; AC2 PROVE (grep for string presence) still passes even if key inventory is wrong or partial

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
